meta_title: For Freelancers: design with AI to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Design with AI to Solve Debugging Issues
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with broken code. Learn how to use Antigravity and Claude Code to design with AI, ship faster, and deliver professional results for clients.
content.answer_block: When debugging stalls, pivot to AI-driven architectural design. By using Antigravity for visual flow modeling and Claude Code for iterative implementation, you bypass manual syntax hunting. This workflow forces structural clarity, allowing AI to generate clean, functional code while you focus on high-level design and rapid client delivery.
content.intro:
You are staring at a screen at 3 AM. The stack trace is an infinite loop of cryptic errors, the client deadline is breathing down your neck, and your momentum has hit a brick wall. This is the freelancer’s tax: spending hours fighting syntax instead of shipping value.

But the paradigm has shifted. Instead of treating code as a manual puzzle, you must treat it as a design problem solved by AI. By moving upstream to architectural design, you stop debugging symptoms and start building systems that work the first time. It is time to stop being a code-monkey and start being a lead architect.

steps.title: Architectural Visualization
steps.description: Map out your logic flow visually to identify breaking points before writing a single line of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the business logic for this feature. Create a visual flow diagram of the data state transitions and identify potential edge cases where the logic might fail.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --architect 'Design the state machine for the requested feature, focusing on error boundary resilience.'

steps.title: Component Decomposition
steps.description: Break the feature into modular, testable components using AI to ensure separation of concerns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Decompose this system into atomic, reusable components. For each component, define the input schema and expected output state.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --decompose 'Break down the project into modular files and draft the interface contracts for each module.'

steps.title: Constraint-Based Implementation
steps.description: Generate code within strict architectural constraints to prevent the 'spaghetti code' that leads to debugging hell.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the implementation for the core service using the previously defined interfaces. Enforce strict type safety and modular design patterns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --implement 'Write the logic for the service layer. Use clean architecture and include unit tests for every critical branch.'

steps.title: Automated Defensive Auditing
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform a proactive audit of your generated code to spot potential runtime errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the generated code for runtime instability. Identify areas where error handling is missing or logic is ambiguous.
steps.claude_code_command: claude audit --fix 'Scan the codebase for potential runtime exceptions and apply defensive programming patches.'

steps.title: Final Integration & Verification
steps.description: Validate the entire system flow and ensure the design matches the client requirements perfectly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Verify the final code against the initial architectural diagram. Generate a summary of how the implementation fulfills the business requirements.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --test 'Run the full integration test suite and provide a coverage report for the implemented module.'

pro_tips: Always define your data schema in a separate YAML file before generating code; this acts as a 'source of truth' for the AI.
Use Claude Code's --chat-history flag to maintain context across multiple sessions, preventing the AI from forgetting your architectural decisions.
If a bug persists, don't ask the AI to 'fix it.' Ask it to 'refactor the module to remove the dependency causing the state conflict.'
Treat your AI prompts as code; store them in a /prompts directory in your repo to ensure repeatability for future client projects.
Force the AI to write unit tests first. If the tests pass, the design is solid. If they fail, your architecture is the problem, not the syntax.
content.next_steps: Begin by auditing your current project's architecture using the Antigravity visualization flow, then implement the core logic using Claude Code to ensure a bug-free foundation.
content.conversion_block: Stop wasting hours in the debugger and start shipping like a senior architect. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
